info.title: AgentGuard Policy Engine API
info.description:
**AgentGuard** is a runtime security platform for AI agents. It provides policy-based evaluation,
audit trails, PII detection, MCP server governance, agent hierarchy management, and OWASP compliance reporting.

## Authentication

AgentGuard uses three authentication schemes:

- **API Key** (`x-api-key` header): Your tenant API key, prefixed `ag_live_`. Used for most authenticated endpoints.
- **Agent Key** (`x-api-key` header): Agent-scoped key, prefixed `ag_agent_`. Used for evaluate calls from specific agents.
- **Admin Key** (`x-admin-key` header or `x-api-key`): Platform admin key. Required for admin-only endpoints.

## Rate Limits

- Unauthenticated: 10 requests/min per IP
- Authenticated: 100 requests/min per IP
- Signup: 5 per hour per IP

## Base URL

Production: `https://api.agentguard.dev/api/v1`

tags.name: Auth
tags.description: Account creation, login, and API key management

tags.name: PII
tags.description: Personally Identifiable Information detection and redaction

tags.name: Agent Hierarchy
tags.description: A2A multi-agent policy propagation and child agent management

tags.name: Playground
tags.description: Interactive policy testing without full auth

ApiKeyAuth.name: x-api-key
ApiKeyAuth.description: Tenant API key (prefixed `ag_live_`) or agent key (prefixed `ag_agent_`)

AdminKeyAuth.name: x-api-key
AdminKeyAuth.description: Platform admin key (set via ADMIN_KEY environment variable)

matchedRuleId.description: ID of the policy rule that matched

piiDetected.description: Whether PII was detected in the request params

apiKey.description: Agent API key (only returned on creation)

policyScope.description: List of tools this agent is scoped to

active.description: Whether the agent is currently active

declaredTools.description: Tools declared by this agent during validation

hash.description: SHA-256 hash of this entry (chained with previous)

entitiesFound.description: Total number of PII entities found

redactedContent.description: Input content with PII replaced by [REDACTED_TYPE] placeholders

get.description: Returns API and database health status. No authentication required.

Returns a step-by-step guide any AI agent can follow to integrate AgentGuard.
No authentication required. This is the zero-friction onboarding path: an agent
reads this endpoint and does the rest.

Returns component-level health status including database latency, uptime,
and version information. Designed for k8s liveness/readiness probes and
monitoring dashboards. No authentication required.

post.description:
Create a new tenant account and receive an API key. The API key is shown **only once** — store it securely.
Rate limited to 5 signups per hour per IP.

apiKey.description: Your API key — store this securely, not shown again

example.message: Account created. Store your API key securely — it will not be shown again.

post.description:
Retrieve an existing tenant's API key using their email address.
This is a recovery mechanism — treat responses carefully.

404.description: No account found for this email

post.description: Generate a new API key and invalidate the current one. The new key is shown only once.

The core evaluation endpoint. Submits an agent action (tool call) to the policy engine
and receives a decision: `allow`, `block`, `monitor`, or `require_approval`.

Authentication is optional — unauthenticated calls use the default policy.
Authenticated calls use tenant-specific custom policies.

sessionId.description: Session identifier for grouping audit events

messageHistory.description: Recent conversation history for context-aware detection

value.reason: Shell execution is blocked by security policy

post.description:
Evaluate up to 50 tool calls in a single request. Each call is evaluated in parallel
against the same effective policy. Individual call failures do not fail the batch.
Returns per-call decisions plus an aggregate summary.

sessionId.description: Session identifier for grouping audit events

results.reason: Shell execution is blocked by security policy
results.suggestion: Use a safer alternative like file_read or file_write

post.description:
Register a new agent and receive a scoped agent API key.
Agent keys (prefixed `ag_agent_`) are scoped to this agent's policy and cannot create tenant accounts.

policyScope.description: Tools this agent is allowed to use

example.name: customer-support-bot
example.note: Store the apiKey securely — it will not be shown again.

get.description: List all active agents for the authenticated tenant.
Retrieve details for a specific agent.

post.description:
Dry-run an agent's declared tools through the policy engine to check coverage.
Returns per-tool decisions without logging to the audit trail.
Required before certification.

declaredTools.description: List of tool names the agent will use

post.description:
Mark an agent as certified after achieving 100% policy coverage during validation.
Certified agents appear with a badge in the dashboard.

get.description: Check an agent's current validation coverage and certification status.

ready.description: True if certified and validation coverage is 100%

get.description:
Retrieve paginated audit trail entries for the authenticated tenant.
Entries are chained with SHA-256 hashes for tamper detection.

chainValid.description: Whether the hash chain is valid

get.description:
Retrieve audit events using cursor-based pagination. More efficient than offset-based
pagination for large audit trails. Use the `nextCursor` value from the response as the
`before` parameter in subsequent requests.

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: Cursor — return events before this ISO 8601 timestamp

nextCursor.description: Cursor for the next page (null if no more events)

post.description:
Re-compute the hash chain for the tenant's audit trail. Use when hashes are
out of sync due to migration or data import.
Requires admin key.

Register a webhook URL to receive real-time event notifications.
Webhook payloads are HMAC-signed with your optional secret.
Only HTTPS URLs are accepted; private/internal IPs are rejected (SSRF protection).

url.description: HTTPS URL to receive webhook POST requests

secret.description: Optional secret for HMAC signature verification

get.description: List all registered webhooks for the authenticated tenant.

put.description: Update an existing webhook's URL, events, or secret.

get.description: Retrieve the tenant's current effective policy (custom or default).

put.description:
Replace the tenant's custom policy with a new set of rules.
Accepts either a full PolicyDocument or an array of PolicyRule objects.
Maximum 500 rules per policy.

get.description: List all pending human-in-the-loop approval requests for this tenant.

post.description: Approve or reject a pending HITL approval request.

get.description: Retrieve evaluation usage analytics for the past 30 days.

Platform-wide analytics across all tenants.
**Requires admin key.**

Retrieve evaluation usage statistics for the authenticated tenant.
Includes total evaluations, breakdowns by result, last 24h count,
top blocked tools, and average response time.

avgResponseMs.description: Average evaluation response time in milliseconds

post.description: Submit user feedback with a rating (1-5) and optional comment.

comment.example: Great tool, very easy to integrate

agent_id.description: Optional agent this feedback relates to

example.comment: Caught a prompt injection that our tests missed!

get.description:
List all feedback submissions across all tenants.
**Requires admin key.**

post.description:
Anonymous telemetry ping from SDK installations.
No authentication required. Rate limited to 5 per minute per IP.
Always returns 202 (even for invalid payloads) to avoid breaking SDK integrations.

Generate an OWASP Agentic Top 10 compliance report based on the tenant's
audit history and policy configuration. Reports are persisted and retrievable via GET.

agentId.description: Optional — scope report to a specific agent

get.description: Retrieve the most recently generated OWASP compliance report for this tenant.

404.description: No report found — generate one first

get.description: Retrieve a specific OWASP compliance report by its ID.

post.description:
Detect and redact personally identifiable information (PII) from text content.
Detects: email addresses, phone numbers, credit card numbers, SSNs, IP addresses,
dates of birth, names, and more.

Raw PII text is **never** stored — only entity type, position, and confidence score.

content.description: Text content to scan for PII

dryRun.description: If true, scan but don't log to audit trail

post.description:
Evaluate a Model Context Protocol (MCP) tool call against the policy engine with
additional SSRF protection — URL arguments are scanned for private network addresses.

ssrfBlocked.description: True if blocked due to SSRF detection

post.description: Register an MCP server with allowed and blocked tool lists for policy enforcement.

allowedTools.description: Tools explicitly allowed from this server

blockedTools.description: Tools explicitly blocked from this server

get.description: List all registered MCP servers for this tenant.

post.description:
Configure Slack HITL (Human-in-the-Loop) integration.
When an evaluation returns `require_approval`, a Slack message is sent
with Approve/Reject buttons. The response is captured via the callback endpoint.

signingSecret.description: Slack app signing secret for HMAC verification

autoRejectMinutes.description: Auto-reject pending approvals after this many minutes

get.description: Retrieve current Slack integration settings. Signing secret is redacted.

delete.description: Remove the Slack HITL integration configuration.

post.description:
Endpoint for receiving Slack interactive component payloads (button clicks).
**Not for direct use** — this is called by Slack when users click Approve/Reject buttons.
Verifies Slack HMAC signature before processing.

Create a child agent with inherited (or more restrictive) policy from the parent.
Policy inheritance rules:
- `blockedTools`: union of parent + child (monotonically restrictive)
- `allowedTools`: intersection of parent + child
- `hitlTools`: union of parent + child
- TTL and maxToolCalls are enforced by the parent's limits

allowedTools.description: Tools this child agent may use (intersected with parent's allowed list)

blockedTools.description: Tools explicitly blocked for this child

hitlTools.description: Tools requiring human approval for this child

maxToolCalls.description: Maximum number of tool calls allowed

get.description: List all child agents spawned by the specified parent agent.

post.description:
Create an in-memory playground session for testing policy evaluation without
full tenant setup. Optionally provide a custom policy for the session.
Sessions are ephemeral — they are not persisted to the database.

policy.description: Optional custom policy document for this session

get.description:
List all available pre-built policy templates. Templates provide curated rule sets
for common compliance frameworks (OWASP, SOC2, EU AI Act, APRA CPS234, Financial Services).
Retrieve a specific policy template by its ID.

200.description: Template details with full rule set

post.description:
Apply a policy template to this tenant's configuration. This records the application
in the audit trail and provides the rules for integration into your policy engine.

Activate or deactivate the tenant-level kill switch.
When active, all evaluations for this tenant return `block` immediately.
This is an emergency stop mechanism for incident response.

active.description: Set to true to activate, false to deactivate. Omit to toggle.

example.message: Tenant kill switch ACTIVATED — your evaluations will return BLOCK

post.description:
Pre-flight admission check for an MCP server before connecting it to an agent.
Evaluates each tool the server exposes against the current policy engine and
returns a per-tool admission decision.

serverUrl.description: URL of the MCP server being admitted

description.example: Read a file from the filesystem

inputSchema.description: JSON Schema for the tool's input parameters

admitted.description: True only if all tools are admitted (allowed or monitored)

results.reason: File deletion is blocked by security policy
